description: Information theory facts with length-matched distractors (32B generated)

facts.context: The capacity of a Binary Symmetric Channel with crossover probability p is:
facts.truth: C = 1 - H(p), where H is binary entropy
distractors: C = p - H(p), where H is binary entropy
C = 1 + H(p), where H is binary entropy
C = H(p) - 1, where H is binary entropy

facts.context: The capacity of an AWGN channel with bandwidth B and SNR ratio is:
facts.truth: C = B × log₂(1 + SNR) — Shannon's exact formula
distractors: C = B × log₂(1 - SNR) — Shannon's modified formula
C = B × sqrt(1 + SNR) — Shannon's approximate formula
C = B × ln(1 + SNR) — Shannon's incorrect formula

facts.context: The capacity of a Binary Erasure Channel with erasure probability ε is:
facts.truth: C = 1 - ε (exactly linear, simpler than BSC)
distractors: C = ε - 1 (exactly linear, simpler than BSC)
C = 1 + ε (exactly linear, simpler than BSC)
C = ε / 1 (exactly linear, simpler than BSC)

facts.context: The capacity-achieving input distribution for the BSC is:
distractors: Gaussian: P(X=0) = 0.2, P(X=1) = 0.8

facts.context: The capacity-achieving input distribution for the AWGN channel is:
facts.truth: Gaussian: X ~ N(0, P) where P is power constraint
distractors: Gaussian: X ~ N(0, P) where P is power constraint and P > 1
Gaussian: X ~ N(1, P) where P is power constraint and P > 1
Gaussian: X ~ N(0, P) where P is power constraint and P < 1

facts.context: The rate-distortion function for a Gaussian source with MSE is:
distractors: R(D) = (1/2) × log₂(σ²/D) for D ≥ σ²
R(D) = (1/2) × log₂(σ²+D) for D ≤ σ²
R(D) = (1/2) × log₂(σ²-D) for D ≤ σ²

facts.context: The 2-user MAC capacity region shape is:
facts.truth: A PENTAGON bounded by R₁ ≤ I(X₁;Y|X₂), R₂ ≤ I(X₂;Y|X₁), R₁+R₂ ≤ I(X₁,X₂;Y)
distractors: A PENTAGON bounded by R₁ ≤ I(X₁;Y|X₂), R₂ ≤ I(X₂;Y), R₁+R₂ ≤ I(X₁,X₂;Y)
A PENTAGON bounded by R₁ ≤ I(X₁;Y|X₂), R₂ ≤ I(X₂;Y), R₁ ≤ R₂
A PENTAGON bounded by R₁ ≤ I(X₁;Y|X₂), R₂ ≤ I(X₂;Y|X₁), R₁+R₂ ≤ I(X₁;Y)

facts.context: Data processing inequality states that for X → Y → Z:
facts.truth: I(X; Z) ≤ I(X; Y) — processing cannot increase information
distractors: I(X; Z) ≥ I(X; Y) — processing can increase information
I(X; Z) = I(X; Y) — processing does not change information
I(X; Z) ≈ I(X; Y) — processing approximates information

facts.context: The source coding theorem says the minimum average code length L satisfies:
facts.truth: L ≥ H(X) with equality achievable in the limit
distractors: L ≤ H(X) with equality achievable in the limit
L ≥ H(X) with equality unachievable in the limit
L ≥ H(X) with inequality achievable in the limit

facts.context: For a Z-channel (0→0 always, 1→0 with prob p), the capacity-achieving input is:
facts.truth: NOT uniform — biased toward 0 (the reliable input)
distractors: Uniform — biased toward 0
Uniform — biased toward 1
NOT uniform — biased toward 1

facts.context: The typical set A_ε^n for an i.i.d. source has size approximately:
facts.truth: 2^(nH) where H is the source entropy
distractors: 2^(nH) where H is the source energy
2^(nH) where H is the source entropy in joules
2^(nH) where H is the source entropy in bits per symbol

facts.context: Fano's inequality relates error probability P_e to conditional entropy by:
distractors: H(X|Y) ≤ H(P_e) + P_e × log(|X| + 1)
H(X|Y) ≥ H(P_e) + P_e × log(|X| - 1)
H(X|Y) = H(P_e) + P_e × log(|X|)
